list.title: App#addPlugin
list.description: Add and save plugin and it's base class definition (if provided).

list.title: App#applet
list.description: If app runs in applet mode, this will be the applet's name.

list.title: App#applets
list.description: Applets container.

list.title: App#args
list.description:
Program arguments.

$ node index.js arg1 arg2
...
console.log(this.args) // it should print: ['arg1', 'arg2']

list.title: App#argv
list.description:
Program options.

  • Dash (-) breaks the string into object keys
  • While colon (:) is used as namespace separator. If no namespace found, it is saved under _ key.

Values are parsed automatically. See {@link https://github.com/ladjs/dotenv-parse-variables|dotenv-parse-variables} for details.

$ node index.js --my-name-first=John --my-name-last=Doe --my-birthDay=secret --nameSpace:path-subPath=true
...
// {
//   _: {
//    my: {
//       name: { first: 'John', last: 'Doe' },
//       birthDay: 'secret'
//     }
//   },
//   nameSpace: { path: { subPath: true } }
// }

list.title: App#baseClass
list.description:
All plugin's base class are saved here as key-value pairs with plugin name as its key. The special key Base && Tools is for {@link Base} & {@link Tools} class so anytime you want to create your own plugin, just use something like this:

class MyPlugin extends this.app.baseClass.Base {
  ... your class
}

list.title: App#boot
list.description:
Boot process:

  • Parsing {@link module:Lib.parseArgsArgv|program arguments, options} and {@link module:Lib.parseEnv|environment values}
  • Create {@link Bajo|Bajo} instance & initialize it
  • {@link module:Helper/Bajo.runAsApplet|Run in applet mode} if -a or --applet is given

After boot process is completed, event bajo:afterBootCompleted is emitted.

If app mode is applet, it runs your choosen applet instead.

list.title: App#boxen
list.description: Placeholder for boxen that will get imported from bajoCli later during boot process.

list.title: App#configHandlers
list.description:
Config handlers.

By default, there are two built-in handlers available: .js, .json and .yml/.yaml. Use plugins to add more, e.g {@link https://github.com/ardhi/bajo-config|bajo-config} lets you to use .toml.

list.title: App#dump
list.description:
Dumping variable on screen. Like console.log with configurable options. Useful for quick debugging and testing. You can also use it to dump variables in production without worrying about performance because it is using Bajo's built-in cache to store the result of util's inspect, so it will only be processed once for each unique variable.

Any argument passed to this method will be displayed on screen. If the last argument is a boolean true, app will quit rightaway after dumping.

If you have bajoCli plugin installed, variables will be displayed in a nice box using boxen package. Otherwise, it will fallback to console.log with util's inspect result.

To have more control on how the variable is displayed, you can set options in Bajo's config under dump key. See {@link Bajo#config} for details.

list.title: App#envVars
list.description:
Environment variables. Support dotenv (.env) file too!

  • Underscore (_) translates key to camel-cased one
  • Double underscores (__) breaks the key into object keys
  • While dot (.) is used as namespace separator. If no namespace found, it is saved under _ key.

Values are also parsed automatically using {@link https://github.com/ladjs/dotenv-parse-variables|dotenv-parse-variables}.

Example:

  • MY_KEY=secret → { _: { myKey: 'secret' } }
  • MY_KEY__SUB_KEY=supersecret → { _: { myKey: { subKey: 'supersecret' } } }
  • MY_NS.MY_NAME=John → { myNs: { myName: 'John' } }

list.title: App#exit
list.description: Terminate the app and back to console.

list.title: App#getAllNs
list.description: Get all loaded plugin namespaces.

list.title: App#getAllPlugins
list.description: Get all plugins loaded plugins.

list.title: App#getConfigFormats
list.description: Helper method to list all supported config formats.

list.title: App#getPlugin
list.description: Get plugin by its namespace.

list.title: App#getPluginDataDir
list.description: Get plugin data directory

list.title: App#getPluginFile
list.description:
Resolve file path from:

  • local/absolute file
  • TNsPath (myPlugin:/path/to/file.txt)
  • file under node_modules, e.g. myPlugin:node_modules/some-package/file.txt

list.title: App#getPlugins
list.description: Get loaded plugins.

list.title: App#lib
list.description:
Gives you direct access to the most commonly used 3rd party library in a Bajo based app. No manual import necessary, always available, anywhere, anytime!

Example:

const { camelCase, kebabCase } = this.app.lib._
console.log(camelCase('Elit commodo sit et aliqua'))

list.title: App#loadIntl
list.description: Load internationalization & languages files for particular plugin.

list.title: App#log
list.description: Instance of system log.

list.title: App#options
list.description: Copy of provided options.

list.title: App#pluginPkgs
list.description: Plugin's package names container. This is the list of plugins to load. It is read from package.json and .plugins file by default, but you can override it by providing options.plugins at constructor.

list.title: App#runAt
list.description: Date/time when your app start.

list.title: App#startPlugin
list.description: Start a plugin.

list.title: App#stopPlugin
list.description: Stop a plugin.

list.title: App#t
list.description:
Translate text and interpolate with given args.

There is a shortcut to this method attached on all plugins. You'll normally call that shorcut instead of this method, because it is bound to plugin's name already

... within your main plugin
const translated = this.app.t('main', 'My cute cat is %s', 'purring')
// or
const translated = this.t('My cute cat is %s', 'purring')

list.title: App#te
list.description: Check whether translation text/key exists

list.title: App.this.envs
list.description: App environments.

list.title: App.this.mainNs
list.description: Your main namespace. And yes, you suppose to NOT CHANGE this.

list.title: Bajo#breakNsPath
list.description: Break name to its namespace & path infos.

list.title: Bajo#buildCollections
list.description: Method to transform config's array or object into an array of collection.

list.title: Bajo#buildNsPath
list.description: Build ns/path pairs.

list.title: Bajo#callHandler
list.description:
Calling any plugin's method by its name:

  • If name is a string, the corresponding plugin's method will be called with passed args as its parameters
  • If name is a plugin instance, this will be used as the scope instead. The first args is now the handler name and the rest are its parameters
  • If name is a function, this function will be run under scope with the remaining args
  • If name is an object and has handler key in it, this function handler will be instead

list.title: Bajo#config
list.description: Config object.

list.title: Bajo#eachPlugins
list.description:
This function iterates through all loaded plugins and call the provided handler scoped as the running plugin. And an object with the following key serves as its parameter:

  • file: file matched the glob pattern
  • dir: plugin's base directory

list.title: Bajo#format
list.description: Format value.

list.title: Bajo#formatByType
list.description: Format value by type.

list.title: Bajo#fromJs
list.description: Read and parse JavaScript file.

list.title: Bajo#fromJson
list.description: Read and parse JSON string or object.

list.title: Bajo#fromYaml
list.description: Parse YAML text

list.title: Bajo#fromYml
list.description: Parse YML text. Alias for fromYaml.

list.title: Bajo#getMethod
list.description: Get class method by name.

list.title: Bajo#getModuleDir
list.description: Get module directory.

list.title: Bajo#getUnitFormat
list.description: Get unit format.

list.title: Bajo#importModule
list.description:
Import file/module from any loaded plugins.

Method proxy from {@link module:Lib.importModule}

list.title: Bajo#importPkg
list.description:
Import one or more packages belongs to a plugin.

If the last arguments passed is an object, this object serves as options object:

  • returnDefault: should return package's default export. Defaults to true
  • throwNotFound: should throw if package is not found. Defaults to false
  • noCache: always use fresh import. Defaults to false
  • asObject: see below. Defaults to false

Return value:

  • if options.asObject is true (default false), return as object with package's names as it's keys
  • Otherwise depends on how many parameters are provided, it should return the named package or an array of packages

Example: you want to import delay and chalk from bajo plugin because you want to use it in your code

const { importPkg } from this.app.bajo
const [delay, chalk] = await importPkg('bajo:delay', 'bajo:chalk')

await delay(1000)
...

list.title: Bajo#init
list.description:
Initialization:

  1. Building {@link module:Helper/Bajo.buildBaseConfig|base config}
  2. {@link module:Helper/Bajo.buildPlugins|Building plugins}
  3. Collect all {@link module:Helper/Bajo.collectConfigHandlers|config handler}
  4. Building {@link module:Helper/Bajo.buildExtConfig|extra config}
  5. Setup {@link module:Helper/Bajo.bootOrder|boot order}
  6. {@link module:Helper/Bajo.bootPlugins|Boot loaded plugins}
  7. Attach {@link module:Helper/Bajo.exitHandler|exit handlers}

list.title: Bajo#isLogInRange
list.description: Check whether log level is within log's app current level.

list.title: Bajo#isValidApp
list.description: Check whether directory is a valid Bajo app.

list.title: Bajo#isValidPlugin
list.description: Check whether directory is a valid Bajo plugin.

list.title: Bajo#join
list.description: Human friendly join array of items.

list.title: Bajo#numUnit
list.description: Return its numeric portion of a value.

list.title: Bajo#readAllConfigs
list.description: Read all config files from path.

list.title: Bajo#readAsConfig
list.description: Read config using all registered config handlers. The first handler that returns a valid object or array will be used.

list.title: Bajo#readConfig
list.description:
Read and parse file as config object. Supported types: .js, .json and .yml/.yaml. More supports can be added using plugin. {@link https://github.com/ardhi/bajo-config|bajo-config} gives you additional supports for .yml, .yaml and .toml file.

If file extension is .*, it will be auto detected and parsed accordingly

list.title: Bajo#readJson
list.description: Read and parse json file.

list.title: Bajo#runHook
list.description: Run named hook/event.

list.title: Bajo#saveAsDownload
list.description:
Save item as file in Bajo's download directory. That is a directory inside your Bajo plugin's data directory.

If file exists already, file will automatically be renamed incrementally.

list.title: Bajo#toJson
list.description: Convert data to JSON string.

list.title: Bajo#toYaml
list.description: Convert object to YAML string

list.title: Bajo#toYml
list.description: Convert object to YML string. Alias for toYaml.

list.title: Base#dispose
list.description: Dispose internal references.

list.title: Base#exit
list.description: Upon app termination, this method will be called first. Mostly useful for system cleanup, delete temporary files, freeing resources etc.

list.title: Base#init
list.description: After config is read, plugin will be initialized. You can still change your config here, because after plugin is initialized, config will be deep frozen.

list.title: Base#loadConfig
list.description: Load config from file in data directory, program arguments and environment variables. Level of importance: Env Variables > Program Arguments > Config File

list.title: Base#start
list.description: This method will be called after plugin's init

list.title: Base.this.dependencies
list.description:
Dependencies to other plugins. Enter all plugin's package name your plugin dependent from.

Semver is also supported.

list.title: Cache#_purgeItem
list.description: Remove a specific cache namespace or all first-level namespaces.

list.title: Cache#sync

list.title: Log#app
list.description: The app instance.

list.title: Log#debug
list.description: Display & format message in debug level. See {@link Log#formatMsg|formatMsg} for details.

list.title: Log#dispose
list.description: Dispose internal references.

list.title: Log#error
list.description: Display & format message in error level. See {@link Log#formatMsg|formatMsg} for details.

list.title: Log#fatal
list.description: Display & format message in fatal level. See {@link Log#formatMsg|formatMsg} for details.

list.title: Log#formatMsg
list.description:
Display & format message according to one of these rules:

  1. level prefix text var 1 var 2 ...var n - Translate text and interpolate with vars for level level
  2. level prefix data text var 1 var 2 ...var n - As above, and append stringified data
  3. level prefix error - Format as {@link Err} object. If current log level is trace, dump it on screen

In prod environment, log will be delivered as JSON stringified object. See {@link TLogJson} for more info

list.title: Log#getRotationPattern
list.description: Calculate pattern used for log rotation.

list.title: Log#info
list.description: Display & format message in info level. See {@link Log#formatMsg|formatMsg} for details.

list.title: Log#save
list.description: Save log to file in {dataDir}/log.

list.title: Log#silent
list.description: Display & format message in silent level. See {@link Log#formatMsg|formatMsg} for details.

list.title: Log#trace
list.description: Display & format message in trace level. See {@link Log#formatMsg|formatMsg} for details.

list.title: Log#warn
list.description: Display & format message in warn level. See {@link Log#formatMsg|formatMsg} for details.

list.title: bajo:afterAll{method}
list.description: Run after all {method} executed. Accepted {method}: Init or Start

list.title: bajo:afterBootComplete
list.description: Run after boot process is completed

list.title: bajo:afterBuildCollection
list.description: Run after collection is built

list.title: bajo:afterCollectHooks
list.description: Run after hooks are collected

list.title: bajo:beforeAll{method}
list.description: Run before all {method} executed. Accepted {method}: Init or Start

list.title: bajo:beforeBuildCollection
list.description: Run before collection is built.

list.title: boot
list.description:
Main entry point of a Bajo app. Returned value is the app instance itself.

While you can code directly around it, you typically write your code the main plugin ({mainNs}) or writing your own custom plugin.

I recommend the second method for its portability.

list.title: event:{ns}:after{method}
list.description:
Run after {method} is executed within {ns} context

  • {ns} - namespace
  • {method} - Accepted methods: Init or Start

list.title: event:{ns}:beforeAppletRun
list.description: Run before applet is run. [ns] is applet's namespace

list.title: event:{ns}:before{method}
list.description:
Run before {method} is executed within {ns} context

  • {ns} - namespace
  • {method} - Accepted methods: Init or Start

list.title: module:Helper
list.description: Internal helpers called by Bajo and other classes. It should remains hidden and not to be imported by any program.

list.title: module:Helper.exports.bootOrder
list.description: Setup plugins boot orders by reading plugin's .bootorder file if provided.

list.title: module:Helper.exports.bootPlugins
list.description:
Iterate through all plugins loaded and do:

  1. {@link module:Helper/Base.buildConfigs|build configs}
  2. {@link module:Helper/Base.checkNameAliases|ensure names & aliases uniqueness}
  3. {@link module:Helper/Base.checkDependencies|ensure dependencies are met}
  4. {@link module:Helper/Base.collectHooks|collect hooks}
  5. {@link module:Helper/Base.run|run plugins}

list.title: module:Helper.exports.buildBaseConfig
list.description:
Building bajo base config. Mostly dealing with directory setups:

  • determine base directory
  • check whether data directory is valid. If not exist, create one inside app dir
  • ensure data config directory is there
  • ensure tmp dir is there
  • read the list of plugins from .plugins file

list.title: module:Helper.exports.buildConfigs
list.description: Build configurations

list.title: module:Helper.exports.buildExtConfig
list.description:
Bajo extra config:

  • reading config file
  • merge config with arguments & environments values
  • Set environment (dev or prod)

list.title: module:Helper.exports.buildPlugins
list.description:
Building all plugins:

  • load from app's pluginPkgs
  • iterate through the list and build related plugins
  • making sure main plugin is there. If not, create from template
  • attach these plugins to the app instance

list.title: module:Helper.exports.checkDependencies
list.description: Ensure dependencies are met

list.title: module:Helper.exports.checkNameAliases
list.description: Ensure for names and aliases to be unique and no clashes with other plugins

list.title: module:Helper.exports.collectConfigHandlers
list.description: Collect all config handlers, including the one provided by plugins

list.title: module:Helper.exports.collectHooks
list.description: Collect and build hooks and push them to the bajo's hook system

list.title: module:Helper.exports.exitHandler
list.description: Attach plugins exit handlers and make sure the app shutdowns gracefully

list.title: module:Helper.exports.run
list.description: Finally, run all plugins

list.title: module:Helper.exports.runAsApplet
list.description: If app is in applet mode, this little helper should take care plugin's applet boot process

list.title: module:Helper{ns}:afterAppletRun
list.description: Run after applet is run. [ns] is applet's namespace

list.title: module:Lib
list.description: External helpers you can use by importing its file directly

list.title: module:Lib.findDeep
list.description: Find item deep in paths.

list.title: module:Lib.freeze
list.description: Freeze object.

list.title: module:Lib.importModule
list.description:
Import file/module from any loaded plugins.

E.g. your plugin structure looks like this:

|- src
|  |- lib
|  |  |- my-module.js
|- index.js
|- package.json

And this is how to import my-module.js:

const { importModule } = this.app.bajo
const myModule = await importModule('myPlugin:/src/lib/my-module.js')
